exercises.title: SCAN RANGE
exercises.brief: Sweep every sector from a to b.
promptLines: Define **rangeArr(a, b)** returning an **array** of the integers from `a` up to `b`, **inclusive** (assume `a <= b`). Build it with a loop.
starterLines: // TODO: push i from a through b into an array
solutionLines: for (let i = a; i <= b; i++) { out.push(i); }
exercises.hint: Start out = [], then for (let i = a; i <= b; i++) out.push(i).
exercises.lore: Paint the grid one sector at a time. Miss nothing.

exercises.title: TALLEST SPIKE
exercises.brief: Track the biggest reading in one pass.
promptLines: Define **maxOf(nums)** returning the largest number in the array. Do **not** use `Math.max`; walk the array with a loop. An **empty** array returns **null**.
starterLines: // TODO: track the biggest value seen so far
solutionLines: for (const n of nums) { if (n > best) { best = n; } }
exercises.hint: Guard the empty case with null, seed best = nums[0], then raise it whenever n > best.
exercises.lore: Somewhere in the noise, one spike towers. Pin it.

exercises.title: SIGNAL TALLY
exercises.brief: Count how often the target shows up.
promptLines: Define **countMatches(arr, target)** returning how many elements **strictly equal** `target` (`===`). Count them with a loop.
starterLines: // TODO: count elements equal to target
solutionLines: for (const x of arr) { if (x === target) { count++; } }
exercises.hint: Start count = 0, loop with for...of, and count++ whenever x === target.
exercises.lore: How many times did that ghost ping the net? Count every hit.

exercises.title: DIGIT CRUSH
exercises.brief: Fold a number down to its digit sum.
promptLines: Define **digitsSum(n)** returning the sum of the decimal digits of a non-negative integer `n`. Use a **while** loop with `% 10` and `Math.floor(n / 10)` — no string conversion.
starterLines: // TODO: peel off n % 10 while n > 0

tests.name: zero -> 0
tests.code: assertEqual(digitsSum(0), 0, 'the loop should not run for 0');

exercises.hint: Loop while n > 0: add n % 10 to the sum, then n = Math.floor(n / 10).
exercises.lore: Crush the address down to a single resonance. The grid hums back.

exercises.title: BUZZ GRID
exercises.brief: Stamp the classic fizz/buzz pattern across a range.
promptLines: Define **fizzList(n)** returning an **array** for `1..n`: multiples of **15** become `'FB'`, multiples of **3** become `'F'`, multiples of **5** become `'B'`, otherwise the number itself. `fizzList(0)` is `[]`.
exercises.hint: Check i % 15 first, then i % 3, then i % 5, else push the number — order matters.
exercises.lore: Three and five collide on fifteen. The grid lights up double.
